entry.title: Check Settlement Rule is not blank when release project
entry.content: Hello, We use SAP S/4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD 2408. We want to check settlement rule is not blank when release project (APP: Project Control - Enterprise Projects ) . We find 2 custom logic: /CPD/ENGMT_PROJ_SAVE & /S4PPM/PROJECT_VALIDATION, but it seems unable to meet our needs. I found a similar question link ( https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/checking-if-settlement-rule-is-created-before-releasing-project/qaq-p/4624750 ), it is said here that a custom status can be used, but there's no configuration in public cloud. Could anybody can help me? Thanks a lot. Best Regards, John Huang

entry.title: Re: Integration of SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA to CALM Project and Task Management (PTM)
entry.content:
Hi Astrid, at the beginning of this blog it says:

"Since July (2023) it has been possible for customers to process their SAP Readiness Check results in their own SAP Cloud ALM tenant. Now, customers can leverage an extended integration option for SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA upgrades with SAP Cloud ALM for Project and Task Management."

At the end of this blog, it says: "The integration of SAP Readiness Check results is currently available for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA upgrade analyses and is limited to private (!) SAP Cloud ALM tenants."

What is not really clear to me (and there is such a question  I found unanswered):

Are ECC 6.0 instalments entitled to use this for their Readiness check (I would assume that they do not have a SAP Cloud ALM tenant - and if not: Is it possible to acquire it ?)

And: If ECC 6.0 customers can acquire it: There is currently no SAP Cloud ALM tenant for the intended migration to SAP S/4HANA Cloud PublicEdition - correct?

Thank you & kind regards,

Paul

ご存じの方がいましたらご回答よろしくお願いいたします。

entry.title: Re: Bulk transfer of equipment to new functional location
entry.content:
Hi Prateek,

Since there's no standard application support mass install/dismantle of equipment/functional location.

I would recommend you to take a look on API_EQUIPMENT with entity InstallEquipment or DismantleEquipment which can be used for installation or dismantle. And for mass operation, you may try with BATCH request or Collection run with POSTMAN.

API documentation for your reference:

Install or Dismantle Equipment

Best Regards,

Nick

entry.title: Cloud foundry Runtimeのメモリ拡張について
entry.content:
BTPのCloudfoundry Runtimeサービスを使用して、複数のJavaアプリケーションを実行する予定です。
各API毎に１つのアプリケーションとして開発したいと考えています。
1つのCloudfoundry Runtimeインスタンスで複数のJavaアプリケーションを管理することは可能ですか？

また、「このくらいのアプリケーション規模ならメモリはこのぐらい割り当てるべき」
というようなベンチマークとして利用できる情報やウェブサイトはありますか？
ご存じの方がいましたら、ご回答のほどよろしくお願いいたします。

entry.title: Add additional recipients to the email notification when a Purchase Requisition is approved
entry.content:
In S4/HANA Public cloud, the email is sent by default to the requestor only. How can we add additional recipients for the email notification when the PR is approved? I can't seem to find any available BAdi for this requirement. 

Edit: The Notification tab no longer exists in the current version of the Manage Workflow app. It seems like this is where we can modify the recipients of the email notifications. Do we have a separate app now where we can modify this? Or is this function really gone now?

entry.title: In Ariba, can we load the payment terms onto the supplier partitioned record via supplier loads?
entry.content:
We currently use the "Payment Terms file" that SAP and Ariba suggests, but this file comes with challenges and our terms in Ariba are not matching up with the actual SAP payments.   Our users can only use Ariba to find out the scheduled payment date, so this data has to be accurate.

Many of our users do work for all of North America, so having them pinned down to US or CN within their profile does not work very well.  

I see in the documentation, that you can load the payment net terms onto the partitioned record if you are not using SAP/S4/Hana, but I want to know if anyone has had success with this request?

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Elements - Issue with EditFlow (onBeforeCre...
entry.content:
Sure, this is the code in onBeforeCreate method

override: {
      editFlow: {
                onBeforeCreate: function () {
                    //display a popup
                    var oResourceBundle = this.base.getView().getModel("i18n").getResourceBundle();
                    var that = this;
                    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                        var oInput = new sap.m.Input({
                            placeholder: oResourceBundle.getText("reqTypePlaceholder"),
                            tooltip: oResourceBundle.getText("reqTypeTooltip"),
                            type: sap.m.InputType.Number
                        });
                        var oDialog = new sap.m.Dialog({
                            title: oResourceBundle.getText("reqTypeDialog"),
                            content: [
                                new sap.ui.layout.form.SimpleForm({
                                    editable: true,
                                    layout: "ResponsiveGridLayout",
                                    maxContainerCols: 2,
                                    content: [new sap.m.Label({
                                        required: true,
                                        text: oResourceBundle.getText("reqTypeLabel")
                                    }),
                                        oInput]
                                })
                            ],
                            buttons: [
                                new sap.m.Button({
                                    text: oResourceBundle.getText("proceedButtonTxt"),
                                    press: function () {
                                        // Check if the input field has a value
                                        if (oInput.getValue().trim() !== "") {
                                            that._ReqType = oInput.getValue();
                                            resolve(oInput.getValue());  // Resolve with input value
                                            oDialog.close();
                                        } else {
                                            // Indicate an error if input is empty
                                            MessageToast.show(oResourceBundle.getTexT("reqTypeValidation"));
                                        }
                                    }
                                }),
                                new sap.m.Button({
                                    text: oResourceBundle.getText("cancelButtonTxt"),
                                    press: function () {
                                        reject();  // Reject the Promise on cancel
                                        oDialog.close();
                                    }
                                })
                            ],
                            afterClose: function () {
                                oDialog.destroy();  // Clean up the dialog
                            }
                        });
                        oDialog.open();
                    });
                }
        }
}

entry.title: Currency translation have no data in group reporting module even all prerequisite have been met
entry.content:
Hi all, 

Upon complete configuration for currency translation, we still cannot produce translated results after executing the "Currency Translation" under "Data Monitor" with all below prerequisites are met. The only log show that "the selection should include base unit" which does not look relevant as it is only for currency translation. There are no logs for investigations as well.

I have already done below setup.

  • Maintained translation ratios and exchange rate for "Rate type" = "CLO".
  • Defined exchange rate indicators "C" and assigned to the rate type.
  • Created translation method "S0902" with selection maintained and assigned to consolidation unit which are expected to translate from Local currency to "HKD"
  • Assigned currency translation attribute to the FS items with "S-CT-BS-CLO"

    After execution of currency translation in "Data monitor". No translated data were generated and no relevant log can be shown for reviewing the root cause in error shown in the task details.

    Would anyone know the possible root cause of issue? Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Single Delivery from Multiple Sales Order
Implementing SAP SuccessFactors for 200+ Legal Entities: A Global Transformation Journey
entry.content:
Implementing SAP SuccessFactors Across 200+ Legal Entities: A Comprehensive Solution for Global Operations

In today’s fast-paced business environment, it is crucial for organizations to have a robust Human Resources (HR) management system that aligns with global operations, supports local compliance, and enhances employee experience. As part of our strategic transformation, we successfully implemented SAP SuccessFactors (SF) for over 200 legal entities, utilizing a unified SAP SuccessFactors instance that extends across all affiliates. This integration is a game-changer for our global HR operations and is increasingly becoming a trend among SAP SF customers.

One SAP SuccessFactors Instance for All Affiliates

A significant advantage of our implementation is the use of a single SAP SuccessFactors instance that serves all 200+ affiliates. This is a relatively rare approach among SAP SF customers and sets a high benchmark for global HR management. By using one unified system, we align all affiliates under a common HR platform, ensuring consistent processes, unified data management, and seamless reporting. This structure not only streamlines HR operations but also offers centralized control while maintaining flexibility for local customization.

Aligning with Group Affiliates, Company Policies, and Procedures

Our implementation is designed to align with the corporate structure of the group, ensuring compliance with group-wide policies and procedures while still catering to the unique needs of each affiliate. The SAP SuccessFactors platform provides the flexibility to create global templates and country-specific configurations, making it easier to manage diverse HR practices across regions. This ensures that all legal entities adhere to the same standards, with room for local adaptations, ensuring a balance between global consistency and local compliance.

Diversity and Regional Customization: NA, EMEA, APAC, Japan, China

Given the global nature of our business, the diversity of regions such as North America (NA), Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA), Asia Pacific (APAC), Japan, and China presents unique challenges and opportunities. SAP SuccessFactors allows us to address regional and cultural differences by customizing the system to meet the specific needs of each region, whether it's language preferences, legal requirements, or country-specific HR practices.

  • Global Template: The global template provides the foundation for all affiliates, ensuring consistency in HR processes such as employee management, talent acquisition, performance evaluation, and learning & development.
  • Country-Specific Templates: To address local legal requirements and cultural differences, we have implemented country-specific templates for each affiliate. This ensures compliance with country-specific labor laws, taxation rules, and employment regulations, while maintaining global process consistency.

Single Sign-On (SSO) for All Affiliates with 15 IDPs

To simplify access for all users across 200+ affiliates, we’ve implemented Single Sign-On (SSO) for all users across the organization. With SSO integrated with 15 Identity Providers (IDPs), employees can access the SAP SuccessFactors platform using their corporate credentials. This enhances security, reduces login friction, and provides a seamless user experience. The integration with SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) ensures centralized user management and security policies.

Role-Based Permissions (RBP): Global and Company-Specific Configurations

Role-Based Permissions (RBP) is critical to ensuring that the right individuals have the appropriate access to HR data, based on their roles within the organization. In our SAP SuccessFactors instance, RBP is configured both globally and for specific affiliates to ensure compliance with company policies and data privacy regulations.

  • Global RBP: Global role-based access controls ensure that HR data can be managed centrally while adhering to global HR policies.
  • Company-Specific RBP: Specific configurations allow for tailored permissions, ensuring that data access is aligned with local roles, business units, and organizational hierarchies.

Data Privacy Compliance: GDPR and PIPL Laws

Compliance with data privacy regulations is one of the most significant challenges when managing a global workforce. With employees spread across the globe, our SAP SuccessFactors implementation ensures adherence to critical regulations like GDPR and China's Personal Information Protection Law (PIPL).

  • GDPR Compliance for EMEA: In line with GDPR regulations, we have implemented specific restrictions for affiliates in the EMEA region, ensuring that personal data is handled according to local data privacy laws. SAP SuccessFactors provides robust data protection features, allowing us to restrict data access, implement consent management, and manage data retention policies to meet GDPR requirements.
  • China PIPL Compliance: Similarly, for our affiliates in China, we have configured the system to ensure compliance with the China PIPL law. This includes the proper handling of sensitive data and ensuring that employees' personal information is stored and processed in accordance with Chinese data protection laws.

Integration with SAP HCM for 10 Affiliates

Our integration with SAP Human Capital Management (HCM) is a critical aspect of the system, allowing us to synchronize data between SAP SuccessFactors and other legacy systems. This integration ensures a seamless flow of HR data, including employee information, compensation details, and organizational structure, across various SAP HCM modules. Currently, we have integrated SAP SuccessFactors with SAP HCM for 10 affiliates, enabling smooth data exchange and enhancing the accuracy of HR data across the organization.

Interfaces for 100+ Affiliates

In addition to the integration with SAP HCM, we have established interfaces for more than 100 affiliates, ensuring that data from these entities is synchronized with the global SAP SuccessFactors platform. These interfaces support various HR processes such as payroll, performance reviews, and training records, ensuring that all affiliates remain aligned with the global HR strategy.

Integration with SAP SuccessFactors Submodules

We have also integrated various SAP SuccessFactors submodules with our global HR platform, including:

  • Recruiting: For seamless talent acquisition and candidate management.
  • Learning: For tracking and managing employee learning and development.
  • Performance & Goals: To streamline performance evaluations and goal setting across affiliates.
  • Compensation: For managing compensation structures and ensuring pay equity across regions.

These integrations provide a holistic view of employee data and streamline HR processes, enhancing the overall HR experience for both employees and managers.

Reporting: ADHOC, CANVAS, and Story Reports

To support decision-making and performance tracking, we have implemented various reporting tools within SAP SuccessFactors:

  • ADHOC Reports: These allow HR leaders to generate custom reports based on real-time data, empowering them to make informed decisions.
  • CANVAS Reports: SAP SuccessFactors’ CANVAS reporting tool allows for visually rich, interactive reports that are easily customizable to meet the needs of different stakeholders.
  • Story Reports: Story reports offer a dynamic and narrative-driven way to analyze HR data, providing insights that help guide strategic planning and performance evaluations.

Conclusion

By implementing SAP SuccessFactors across 200+ legal entities, we have established a global HR platform that is flexible, compliant with regional regulations, and fully aligned with our organizational goals. This comprehensive system supports everything from global talent management to country-specific HR practices, making it a powerful tool for enhancing collaboration, streamlining workflows, and driving organizational success. The integration of advanced features like SSO, RBP, GDPR, and PIPL compliance, along with robust reporting tools, positions our organization to manage HR operations efficiently and in full compliance with global data privacy standards. As we continue to evolve and expand, SAP SuccessFactors remains a key enabler of our HR transformation journey.

entry.title: Why Companies Moved Out of SAP SuccessFactors and How to Retain Them: A Strategic Approach
entry.content:
Why Companies Moved Out of SAP SuccessFactors: Retaining SAP SuccessFactors Clients and Improving Support

SAP SuccessFactors (SF) has been one of the leading cloud-based HR solutions globally, enabling companies to streamline their HR processes, manage talent, and align their workforce with organizational goals. However, over the past few years, several companies have decided to move away from SAP SuccessFactors after using it for 5 to 10 years. This trend raises questions about why these companies are leaving and what SAP can do to retain them. In this blog, we will explore the reasons behind companies moving out of SAP SuccessFactors, SAP’s strategy for retaining clients, and how businesses can offer better support to their customers.

Why Did Companies Move Out of SAP SuccessFactors?

There are several reasons why some organizations have chosen to transition away from SAP SuccessFactors:

  1. Cost Considerations

As organizations scale or grow, the total cost of ownership (TCO) of SAP SuccessFactors can increase significantly. Some companies find that the cost of SAP’s licenses, maintenance fees, and additional modules can outweigh the benefits they are receiving, especially if they are not fully utilizing all the platform's capabilities.

Example: A mid-sized company might have initially opted for SAP SuccessFactors for its comprehensive modules but later realized that it only needed a basic HR solution, prompting them to switch to a less expensive alternative.

  1. Complexity and Usability Issues

While SAP SuccessFactors offers a broad range of features, some users find it complex and difficult to navigate. The system’s interface, although rich in functionality, can sometimes be unintuitive, leading to user frustration and decreased engagement. Organizations that want simpler, more user-friendly systems may turn to competitors like Workday or BambooHR, which offer more streamlined interfaces.

Example: Employees at an organization could be experiencing slow adoption rates due to the learning curve, which may lead to inefficiencies, prompting them to explore easier-to-use HR platforms.

  1. Customization and Flexibility Concerns

SAP SuccessFactors provides a high level of customization, but in some cases, it may not fully meet the unique needs of certain organizations. For instance, small to mid-sized companies with specific industry requirements may find SAP’s system rigid, making it difficult to tailor the software to their needs.

Example: A growing tech startup might need faster integrations with other tools that are easier to implement in other systems like BambooHR or Oracle HCM Cloud, which may be more agile.

  1. Integration Challenges

While SAP SuccessFactors offers strong integration capabilities, some companies experience challenges when connecting SAP SuccessFactors with their other legacy systems or third-party applications. This can lead to data silos and inefficiencies, causing some organizations to switch to other HR platforms that offer more seamless integration with their ecosystem.

Example: A global organization using SAP HCM may find it difficult to integrate SuccessFactors with regional payroll systems, prompting a migration to a platform with more out-of-the-box integrations.

  1. Customer Support and Response Time

Some companies have reported dissatisfaction with SAP’s customer support, particularly regarding timely responses and effective resolution of issues. In a rapidly changing business environment, organizations need quick and effective support to avoid disruptions. Poor customer support can drive companies to look for alternatives that provide more responsive service.

Example: A company experiencing repeated system downtimes might seek out a competitor offering 24/7 support and faster response times.

How Can SAP Retain These Companies?

SAP has a strong product in SuccessFactors, but to retain customers, it needs to address the concerns that drive organizations to seek alternatives. Here are some strategies SAP can adopt:

  1. Reduce Complexity and Improve User Experience

SAP should focus on simplifying its platform to enhance the user experience. By streamlining the user interface and making it more intuitive, companies can improve engagement and adoption rates. Additionally, offering personalized onboarding and training can help companies get more value from the system.

How to Implement: SAP can create more simplified, industry-specific templates or set up dedicated teams to provide personalized training based on organizational needs.

  1. Lower the Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)

Offering flexible pricing models and transparent licensing can help reduce the overall cost for organizations, making SAP SuccessFactors more accessible for a wider range of companies, especially small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs).

How to Implement: SAP can introduce tiered pricing models or usage-based pricing to allow companies to scale their usage of SuccessFactors as their needs grow.

  1. Enhance Integration Capabilities

SAP should focus on improving the ease and speed of integration with other third-party applications, as well as enhancing its API capabilities. Companies are looking for solutions that can be seamlessly integrated with existing systems and workflows.

How to Implement: SAP can expand its pre-built connectors for popular platforms or enhance its integration hub to simplify the integration process with external systems.

  1. Improve Customer Support and Service

Providing more responsive, proactive, and personalized customer support can go a long way in improving customer satisfaction. SAP should ensure that customers can get timely assistance when facing issues and provide robust self-help resources, including comprehensive knowledge bases and community forums.

How to Implement: SAP can offer dedicated account managers for key clients, as well as quicker access to support agents via live chat or phone support.

  1. Drive Continuous Innovation

Continuous improvement and new features are key to retaining customers. SAP SuccessFactors needs to keep up with changing HR trends, such as AI-driven HR analytics, mobile accessibility, and enhanced employee experience functionalities, to stay competitive in the HR tech space.

How to Implement: SAP should focus on integrating cutting-edge technologies like machine learning and AI to offer predictive analytics and automation for HR tasks.

How Can Businesses Offer Better Support to SAP SuccessFactors Clients?

To better support clients and ensure long-term satisfaction, businesses can:

  • Provide Dedicated Support Teams: Offer specialized support for each client, particularly those with unique configurations or specific needs.
  • Invest in User Training: Ensure that clients have ongoing training and resources to get the most out of SAP SuccessFactors.
  • Conduct Regular Check-Ins: Set up regular meetings to assess the client’s needs, address concerns, and propose potential improvements to their system.

Conclusion:

SAP SuccessFactors remains a powerful tool for HR transformation, but to retain customers, SAP must evolve based on customer needs, focusing on user experience, cost-efficiency, integration capabilities, and customer support. By addressing the reasons why companies are leaving and offering solutions tailored to their requirements, SAP can keep its clients satisfied and help them succeed in a dynamic business environment. Businesses supporting SAP SuccessFactors clients should ensure they are providing top-notch support, personalized service, and continuous improvements to keep customers engaged.

We welcome your comments and suggestions on how to retain SAP SuccessFactors customers and continue to provide them with a valuable business solution.

entry.title: Re: Single Delivery from Multiple Sales Order
entry.content:
Hello @FK5419 

@Fancy_Zou  has provided an answer on how to create a single delivery for multiple sales orders using the Create Outbound Delivery - From Sales Order Fiori application. However, if you still wish to create an outbound delivery for multiple sales orders using transaction VL10H, this is also possible.

Follow these steps:

  1. Select the appropriate delivery creation date and execute.
  2. Choose the sales order numbers, then click on Background. The system will create a single outbound delivery number for multiple sales orders.

Prerequisite:

Ensure that the Order Combination indicator is selected in the sales order, or maintain it in the Customer Master Data for the relevant Business Partner.

I recommend using the application suggested by @Fancy_Zou for a smoother process

entry.title: Re: SAC Advanced Formula - Conditional Check for Existenc...
entry.content:
Just to answer my own question, this is the solution I came up with which is now working:

// Turn off unbooked data generation to ensure only booked (non-empty) data is considered
CONFIG.GENERATE_UNBOOKED_DATA = OFF

// Define the scope for Datasource dimension to include only the relevant values: INPUT, BW, and BW_Override
// This limits calculations to these specific Datasource values for improved efficiency
MEMBERSET [d/PLNHR_DATASOURCE] = ("INPUT", "BW", "BW_Override")

// Loop through each combination of booked records for Employee and Datasource dimensions
FOREACH.BOOKED [d/PLN_EMPLOYEE], [d/PLNHR_DATASOURCE]

    // Check if the current Datasource for this employee is "INPUT"
    IF [d/PLNHR_DATASOURCE] = "INPUT" THEN
        
        // If an INPUT record exists for this employee, copy the records from "BW" to "BW_Override"
        // Set the flag (PLN_FLAG) to "X" on the copied records to indicate they have been overridden
        DATA([d/PLNHR_DATASOURCE] = "BW_Override", [d/PLN_FLAG] = "X") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/PLNHR_DATASOURCE] = "BW")
        
        // Delete the original "BW" records for this employee once they have been copied to "BW_Override"
        DELETE([d/PLNHR_DATASOURCE] = "BW")
        
        // Delete any "BW_FTE" records for this employee as well, as these are no longer needed after override
        DELETE([d/PLNHR_DATASOURCE] = "BW_FTE")
        
    ENDIF

// End of the loop that processes each booked Employee-Datasource combination
ENDFOR

entry.title: Re: Wokzone
entry.content:
Hi Bharath,

Are you seeing the internal backend URL? If so, this likely points to an issue with principal propagation. It may not be properly configured, or the user account you're logged in with doesn't exist on the backend system.

When principal propagation fails (or if the user isn't found on the backend),the system defaults to the next available authentication method, which in this case is SAML.

entry.title: Monitor Database parameter change
entry.content:
How to check if your requirement on Database Monitoring is able to be monitored by Solman System Monitoring or not?

For example: customer requirement is to monitor on "Database Parameter Change". 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Monitor Database parameter change
entry.content:
Check if there's standard metric available in System Monitoring Template. 

  1. Spefify which type of your database system is. 
  2. Tcode: SOLMAN_SETUP
  3. Application Operations
  4. System Monitoring
  5. Step 4 Template Maintenance
  6. Templates --> Database
  7. Select the database type, for example "Oracle"
  8. Tab Metrics, here lists all the standard metrics to be monitored. 

For example, there's no database parameter change related metrics available. In this way, you might need to refer to KBA ##2484872 to create your custom metrics. However, creating custom metrics might need the involvement of your development teams. 

If you require assistance from SAP development to help with the custom development this would be at a chargeable cost, there is more information in the following Note which includes all the relevant links:

11 - Would you like to shape & influence future releases of SAP Products?

Please feel free to let me know if you still got concern on this. Thank you. 

SAP Support

entry.title: TM Scheduling for Stock Transport Orders
entry.content:
Dear friends of TM,

With SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1, we published a first version of our TM based Sales Order Scheduling for scenarios with TM embedded. The feedback of customers and partners was very positive, thanks a lot for that!

But soon questions came up whether we offer TM scheduling also for other business documents to support additional scenarios. Any official statement to that can be found in our roadmap. But with this blog post I want to share my personal experiences when I implemented a prototype to enable TM scheduling for stock transport orders, based on the same scheduling capabilities as for TM based Sales Order Scheduling, in an TM embedded scenario.

So, this is no official guideline or anything like that. Also this is no statement that this functionality will be published soon in standard delivery. It’s just my personal experience, giving some hints how it’s possible to do the implementation without any modification to standard code, just by configuration and development in Z-coding, based on SAP S/4HANA FPS1 resp. FPS2. I also don’t want to give any implementation details, just pointing out on high level how it’s achieved in coding. So, I ask for your understanding that I also can’t discuss your coding or share mine.

But let’s go into details.

Scenario Overview

Basically, we have a process component Business Process Scheduling (BPS) which takes care of the scheduling of relevant dates for several business documents.

So, when we implemented TM based Sales Order Scheduling, we integrated with BPS, which is used by the sales order to schedule delivery date etc. As the stock transport order is also already integrated with BPS, it was luckily not too much of an effort to implement a similar functionality.

In detail we’re dealing with the following scenario:

  1. The requested delivery date from the schedule line of the stock transport order is the input for the BPS, where the calculation of the relevant dates is managed. BPS uses a so-called ‘schema’ to configure which dates are relevant and how start and end dates are determined:
    • Durations of a transport process step are calculated in ‘Determinations’ (-> captured with the prototype described here)
    • Dates and times of transport process steps are calculated in ‘Delegations’ (-> not captured with this prototype, but could be adapted similarly)
  2. Based on the requested delivery date, Transportation Management (TM) can calculate durations resp. dates and times for loading, transport and unloading, ending up in a requested picking date by backward scheduling. From that, BPS calculates a requested material availability date as input for Available-to-Promise (ATP).
  3. ATP confirms a material availability date of the product from which BPS calculates the respective confirmed picking date.
  4. If this confirmed picking date differs from the requested picking date, TM calculates the confirmed delivery date with forward scheduling. Otherwise, the requested delivery date becomes the confirmed delivery date.

For the prototype I used the schema ‘STOCK_TRANSP_DAYS_ADV_ATTRIB’ which is set up in standard as follows:

For the dates being relevant for TM this means:

  • The loading duration is from the start of loading until the goods issue.
  • The transport duration is from goods issue until the delivery at the destination location (which can also be an incoterm location).
  • The unloading duration is from the delivery until the goods receipt.

Of course, it’s also possible to set up a new schema when this does not fit to your scenario.

Configuration of BPS

At first, the stock transport order document needs to be enabled for the scheduling with BPS:

Therefore, you need to add respective entries on plant and storage location level, thereby checking the flag for ‘Shipment Scheduling’.

At next, the ‘BPS Determinations’ must be created and configured in transaction BPS_DETN_CONFIG:

Those ‘BPS Determinations’ are responsible for the determination of the durations of respective activities:

  • They need to be created and the Z-class with the implementation needs to be assigned.
  • The business process ‘Stock Transport’ needs to be assigned.

Now, the created ‘BPS Determinations’ need to be added to the mentioned schema in customizing for the TM relevant activities:

And finally, the stock transport document needs to be activated for BPS by assigning the schema to the stock transport order type and shipping point:

That are the necessary steps in configuration, of course assuming that the ‘regular’ configuration for the stock transport order has already been done.

So, let’s take care of the implementation part.

Implementation

As already mentioned, the implementation can be done without any modification to the standard code, just with local development in Z-namespace.

Therefore, the first step is to create a class which implements an interface from BPS being responsible for handling its 'Determinations':

The parameters of the used interface method ‘DETERMINE’ are as follows:

  • REQUEST: Input parameter from the BPS request as e.g., the request ID
  • ACTIVITY_ATTRIBUTE: Calculated durations are provided to BPS along with each activity
  • DETERMINATION: Name of the current determination

 The basic program flow in method ‘DETERMINE’ needs to be implemented like:

Those steps are explained in the following.

After the initialization with the declaration of parameters, instantiation of attributes etc., the data from the stock transport order needs to be retrieved:

This is done with the help of the ATP buffer, which can be read as shown.

Essential for the scheduling in TM is the logistics integration customizing, which needs to be retrieved for the stock transport order document data:

The Transport Duration Determination Profile controls the TM scheduling where you mention e.g., the planning profile, and the scheduling method as the default route.

As there has been a change from FPS1 to FPS2 in storing the Transport Duration Determination Profile we need to distinguish:

  • For FPS1, the Transport Duration Determination Profile is stored at the Logistics Integration Profile
  • For FPS2, this moved to the TM control Key

So, this needs to be adapted to your particular release.

The toughest part to be implemented might be the determination of the source and destination locations.

  • The source location will in most cases be the supplying plant, or it’s shipping point.
  • The destination location could be the receiving plant or it’s shipping/receiving point. But it could also be an incoterm location.

Based on your scenario, the stock transport order document data needs to be analyzed respectively and this could be pretty simple or a bit more complex…frankly spoken. But surely solvable with a little bit of experience in ABAP, knowledge about the data model of the stock transport order and the handling of locations.

When the locations have been determined, we’re ready to do the TM scheduling, as we know:

  • The source location where the loading takes place with the duration as maintained in the planning profile.
  • The destination location where the unloading takes place with the duration as maintained in the planning profile.
  • The route from source to destination location to derive the transport time.

So, we can call the TM scheduling with the so-called TRS request:

The TRS request needs:

  • BPS request ID -> available with lt_request_ids = request->get_requests( ). 
  • Transport Duration Determination Profile -> Retrieved from Logistics Integration customizing.
  • Source / Destination locations -> Determined from the stock transport order document data.
  • Calculation start date from the BPS request.

The result of the TM scheduling is provided in the ‘TRS result’ which contains the respective durations.

Those now need to be provided back to the BPS for the respective activity:

Hereby, the activities are:

  • /scmtms/if_trs_constants=>sc_trs_activity-load
  • /scmtms/if_trs_constants=>sc_trs_activity-transport
  • /scmtms/if_trs_constants=>sc_trs_activity-unload

 And BPS then finally provides the dates to the stock transport order where those should be reflected in the delivery schedule of the item.

Example

Let’s show this with the following example, given the schema and configuration as shown before:

In the delivery schedule of the stock transport order item we can see the following:

  • We have one day for loading at the sending location. According to the schema, this reflects in the time between the start of loading and the goods issue.
  • The five days of transport reflect in the time between goods issue and delivery at the receiving location.
  • Three days of unloading in the receiving location are shown from delivery to goods receipt.

If the stock transport order is integrated with TM, respective freight units are created, just as you know it. But the integration with TM is not necessary, so this runs also when no freight units are created.

If freight units are created and you do transport planning in TM, the results are not written back to the stock transport order, as we never do that.

So, you see that it’s indeed possible to use the TM scheduling for the stock transport order in an SAP S/4HANA 2023 release, as we already do for the sales order, without modification to the standard code. There are surely some pitfalls in the implementation, but with some experiences in ABAP coding and business background this is surely solvable!

Hope this helps you in your stock transport process to deal with a more accurate planning!

Best regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: KeycloakとSAP BTP間をSSO連携する際のIASの要否
entry.content:
Corporate Identity Provider が Keycloak であれば、
App -> IAS (proxy) -> Keycloak の構成がおすすめです。
ビジネスユーザは Keycloak のパスワードだけ覚えておけばいいです。社外のユーザは Corporate Identity Provider の Keycloak に登録しない運用をする場合が多く、IAS に登録して運用します。
このように将来的な、社内ユーザ、社外ユーザの管理、企業の M&A に対応できるように2階層にしておくと柔軟な運用ができます。

運用で重要なポイントは、シングルサインオンだけでなく、ロールのマッピングです。通常 Identity Provider で Group 属性を管理して、BTP 側で管理しているアプリのロールにマッピングします。

次に重要なポイントは SAML でなくOpenID Connect が業界デフォルトになってきたという点で、業界の動向も捉えて正しい設計をする事です。
https://help.sap.com/whats-new/cf0cb2cb149647329b5d02aa96303f56?Component=Authorization+and+Trust+Management+Service&locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Introduction to Service with Advanced Execution in SAP S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
@sambtr 

Hello,

yes we do support resource-related billing in the process of Service with Advanced Execution. DP90 is the transaction code for the legacy customer service solution, and this is not supported. We have introduced a new transaction code DP92 or you can also use the Fiori app Create Resource-Related Billing Request:

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('DP92')/S29PCE

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('DP92A')/S29PCE

You can already today generate DMR for YBA1 using DP90. Please let us know if you have any further questions around this.

Thanks and Kind Regards,

Abhishek

entry.title: Re: New 2H 2024 SAP Successfactors Time (Tracking) Features
entry.content:
@mogboo 

 

1) Yes, business rules for temporary time information should be possible. If the alert is not coming up, please create a ticket

2) Currently there is no way. But we know we need to provide it - that´s what I mentioned in the blog, the possible fields will be enhanced. But can you give an example why you need it? You can also create an enhancement request - this might speed it maybe a bit up.

3) No, this has indeed been removed. Please create an enhancement request to get those back. Currently there is no other way than to create different time recording profiles.

Best regards

Volker

entry.title: Re: Intercompany matching and reconciliation (ICMR) - per...
entry.content:
@Harry_Vincent FYI, from S/4HANA Cloud 2502 and S/4HANA On-premise 2025, the reconciliation close process in ICMR will be enhanced as follows:

  • When reconciliation for a unit pair is closed, the matching lock, manual assigning/unassigning are on pair level rather than on the leading unit level.
  • Data roll-in and flexible upload are no longer blocked by reconciliation close.
  • In Manage Reconciliation Close, two columns show reconciliation differences: The difference up to latest cutoff timestamp and The difference calculated at the time when the pair was last closed.
  • You can view historical reconciliation statements of each pair on the detail page of Manage Reconciliation Close. 

Thanks!

grace

entry.title: RAP factory action: Response with deep result possible?
entry.content:
Hello everybody,

I implemented a factory action for my OData v4 WebAPI that receives a deep parameter as input. Receiving the structure from parameter, mapping to BO/database structure and persisting works like a charm. I filled the mapped structure with the key values corresponding to the new database entry and I get a response containing the root of my BO. 

Is there a possibility to have a response with a deep structure containing the complete BO including the child structures?

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5, XML: Adding Confirmation Popover for Action (with Parameter)
entry.content:
Hi,

Not sure why are you adding this annotation in your metadata. It must be added in your Local annotation file in UI5 project. 

This works with Target within which you must put the path of your action/function import.

If this doesn't work, you can manually add controller extension and handle the press using your custom code to show the dialog.

BR

Tejas

entry.title: Re: Introduction to Service with Advanced Execution in SAP S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
@MathanINSAP 

Hello,

Service with Advanced Execution process is available and can be configured in the SAP S/4HANA 2022. However, please note that this release has several restrictions and critical gaps and SAP recommends to not use this version for your business. On the other hand, SAP S/4HANA 2023 has covered a lot of these gaps and hence it can be used productively to address a large part of the process / scope.

Please have a look at the following SAP Note:

SAP S/4HANA 2022: Release information and restrictions for Maintenance Service in SAP S/4HANA

Thanks and Kind Regards,

Abhishek

entry.title: ABAP 7.5x Quick Reference
entry.content:
1. Assignments
  2. Data Types
  3. Enumerated Types (7.51)
  4. Virtual Sort (7.52)
  5. Indicators (7.55)
  6. MOVE-CORRESPONDING … EXPANDING NESTED TABLES KEEPING TARGET LINES (7.56)
  7. LOOP AT……STEP n (7.57)
  8. CORRESPONDING Operator Using the Additions MAPPING and DEFAULT (7.58)

1. Asignments

1.1 Numeric calculation assignment (7.54)

AssignmentBefore 7.54Since 7.54

+=,  -=, *=, /=

DATA(num) = 2.

num = num + 1.		DATA(num) = 2.

num += 1.

 

1.2 String assignment (7.54)

AssignmentBefore VersionSince Version
&&=DATA text TYPE string.
text = 'concatenate'.

text = text && | Me!|		DATA text TYPE string.
text = 'Concatenate'.

text &&= | Me!|.

 

1.3 ASSIGN … ELSE UNASSIGN (7.57)

Before 7.57Since 7.57
DATA(field) = 'exists'.

ASSIGN ('field') TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs>).
IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED.
…
ENDIF.

ASSIGN ('nofield') TO <fs>.
IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED
  AND sy-subrc = 0.  "assigned on 2nd assign
…
ENDIF.		DATA(field) = 'exists'.

ASSIGN ('field') TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs>).
IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED.
...
ENDIF.

ASSIGN ('nofield') TO <fs> ELSE UNASSIGN.

IF <fs> IS ASSIGNED.
...
ENDIF.

 

1.4 ASSIGN struct-(comp) (7.57)

Before 7.57Since 7.57

BEGIN OF  tystruc,            
  comp1 TYPE i,  comp2 TYPE i,
END OF tystruc

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <struc> TYPE any, <comp> TYPE any.

ASSIGN struc TO <struc>.
DATA(name) = '<struc>-comp1'.

DO 1000 TIMES. "runs in approx. 60 ms
  ASSIGN  (name) TO <comp>.
ENDDO.

ASSIGN struc TO <struc>.
DATA(name) = 'COMP1'.

DO 1000 TIMES.  "runs in approx. 50 ms
  ASSIGN  <struc>-(name) TO <comp>.
ENDDO.

"Or alternatively

DO 1000 TIMES.    "Runs in approx. 50 ms
  ASSIGN  <struc>-(1) TO <comp>.
ENDDO.

 

1.5 RETURN [expr] for functional methods (7.58)

Before VersionSince Version

METHOD square. "IMPORTING root TYPE i
                          "RETURNING VALUE(r) TYPE i.
 DATA square type i.
   TRY.
     square = ipow( base = root exp = 2 ).
    CATCH cx_sy_arithmetic_error.
     square = 0.
  ENDTRY.

  r = square.
ENDMETHOD.

METHOD square. "IMPORTING root TYPE i
                          "RETURNING VALUE(r) TYPE i.
  TRY.
     RETURN ipow( base = root exp = 2 ).
    CATCH cx_sy_arithmetic_error.
      RETURN 0.
  ENDTRY.

ENDMETHOD.

 

 

2. Data Types

VersionNew Data TypeDescription                                    

7.50

INT8

8-byte integers with signs

Value range: -9,223,372,036,854,775,808 to +9,223,372,036,854,775,807

Output length: 20

7.54

DECFLOAT16

DECFLOAT34

Decimal floating point number

DF16_SCL and DF34_SCL now obsolete

7.54

DATN

Date in internal format of database

Initial value: 0  (DATS has initial value 00000000)

7.54

TIMN

Time in internal format of database

Initial value: 0  (TIMNS has initial value 000000)

7.54

UTCLONG

Time stamp (exact to 100 ns)

Valid Places: 27

Initial value: 0 

7.54

GEOM_EWKB

Geometric data in EWKB representation

Initial value: Empty string



3. Enumerated Types (7.51)

Syntax

TYPES BEGIN OF ENUM enum_type    [STRUCTURE struc]      [BASE TYPE dtype].
  TYPES val1 [VALUE IS INITIAL],
  TYPES val2 [VALUE val],
  TYPES val3 [VALUE val],
  ...
TYPES END OF ENUM enum_type     [STRUCTURE struc].

 Description

Defines a list of allowed values. 

Example 

Syntax checker flags invalid assignment:

TYPES: BEGIN OF ENUM planet,
    mercury,
    venus,
    earth,
    mars,
    jupiter,
    saturn,
    uranus,
    neptune,
END OF ENUM planet.

DATA planet type planet.

planet = earth.
planet = Nebula. "Syntax error: Field "NEBULA" is unknown

 

4. Virtual Sort (7.52)

Syntax

cl_abap_itab_utilities=>virtual_sort( IMPORTING IM_VIRTUAL_SOURCE
                                                                              IM_FILTER_INDEX
                                                         RETURNING RT_VIRTUAL_INDEX )

Description

For the given source table(s) and filter criteria a structure index numbers is returned. These index numbers are in the required order to effectuate the requested sort. This index structure can be used to create a sorted table(s).

Examples

Single Table

For full example see class cl_demo_virtual_sort_simple.

ITAB

COL1COL2COL3COL4
64GI
17DF
69AB
24HB
14AH
43IJ
108EE
21ED
95CG
11GB

v_index

DATA(v_index) = cl_abap_itab_utilities=>virtual_sort(
                             im_virtual_source = VALUE #(( source = REF #( itab )
                             components = VALUE #( ( name = 'col1' )  ( name = 'col2' ) ) ) ) ).

Result:   v_index = (10, 5, 2, 8, 4, 6, 1, 3, 9, 7)

sorted_tab

DATA sorted_tab TYPE itab.
sorted_tab = VALUE #( FOR idx IN v_index ( itab[ idx ] ) ).
COL1COL2COL3COL4
11GB
14AH
17DF
21ED
24HB
43IJ
64GI
69AB
95CG
108EE

 

v_index (re-assigned)

v_index = cl_abap_itab_utilities=>virtual_sort(
                        im_virtual_source = VALUE #(
                              ( source = REF #( itab )
                                components = VALUE #(
                                   ( name = 'col3'
                                     astext = abap_true
                                     descending = abap_true )
                                   ( name = 'col4'
                                     astext = abap_true
                                     descending = abap_true ) ) ) ) ).
Result:   v_index = (6, 4, 1, 10, 7, 8, 2, 9, 5, 3)

sorted_tab (re-assigned)

sorted_tab = VALUE #( FOR idx IN v_index ( itab[ idx ] ) ).
43IJ
24HB
64GI
11GB
108EE

17DF
95CG
14AH
69AB

 

Two tables

For full example see class cl_demo_virtual_sort_combined.

ITAB1

COL1COL2
00
11
11
11
00
10
10
10
00
11

ITAB2

COL1COL2
XX
XX
YY
XY
XX
YY
XY
XY
YX
YY

v_index

v_index = cl_abap_itab_utilities=>virtual_sort(
                     im_virtual_source = VALUE #(
                         ( source = REF #( itab1 )
                           components = VALUE #( ( name = 'col1' )
                                                                   ( name = 'col2' ) ) )
                         ( source = REF #( itab2 )
                           components = VALUE #( ( name = 'col1'
                                                                   astext = abap_true
                                                          descending = abap_true )
                                                                  ( name = 'col2'
                                                                   astext = abap_true
                                                          descending = abap_true ) ) ) ) ).
Result:   v_index = (9, 1, 5, 6, 7, 8, 3, 10, 4, 2)

comb_tab

TYPES:
BEGIN OF test_line,
col11 TYPE i,
col12 TYPE i,
col21 TYPE string,
col22 TYPE string,
END OF test_line,
test_tab TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF test_line WITH EMPTY KEY.
DATA sorted_tab1 TYPE itab1.
sorted_tab1 = VALUE #( FOR idx IN v_index ( itab1[ idx ] ) ).
DATA sorted_tab2 TYPE itab2.
sorted_tab2 = VALUE #( FOR idx IN v_index ( itab2[ idx ] ) ).

FINAL(comb_tab) = VALUE test_tab( FOR i = 1 UNTIL i > 10
                                      ( col11 = sorted_tab1[ i ]-col1
                                        col12 = sorted_tab1[ i ]-col2
                                        col21 = sorted_tab2[ i ]-col1
                                        col22 = sorted_tab2[ i ]-col2 ) ).

COL11COL12COL21COL22
00YX
00XX
00XX
10YY
10XY
10YY
11YY
11YY
11XY
11XX

 

 5. Indicators (7.55)

Syntax

TYPES dtype TYPE struct WITH INDICATORS ind [{TYPE type}].          […AS BITFIELD from 7.56]

UPDATE dbtab FROM TABLE  itab INDICATORS [NOT] SET STRUCTURE set_ind.

Description

When used with “TYPES” it adds a component ind at the end of the structure struc the same number of first-level components.

This can be used together with UPDATE dbtab to only update the components flagged for update.

Example

For full example see class cl_demo_update_set_indicator.

DB table DEMO_UPDATE:

IDCOL1COL2COL3COL4
A1101000
B2202000
C3303000
D4404000
E5505000
F6606000

Internal table ind_tab:

TYPES ind_wa TYPE demo_update WITH INDICATORS col_ind
TYPE abap_bool.
DATA ind_tab TYPE TABLE OF ind_wa.

ind_tab = VALUE #(
    ( id = 'D' col4 = 4000 col_ind-col4 = abap_true )
    ( id = 'E' col4 = 5000 col_ind-col4 = abap_true )
    ( id = 'F' col4 = 6000 col_ind-col4 = abap_true ) ).

IDCOL1COL2COL3COL4COL_IND
D0004000X
E0005000X
F0006000X

Update DB:

UPDATE demo_update FROM TABLE @IND_tab
                                     INDICATORS SET STRUCTURE col_ind.

DB table DEMO_UPDATE:

IDCOL1COL2COL3COL4
A1101000
B2202000
C3303000
D4404004000
E5505005000
F6606006000

 

6. MOVE-CORRESPONDING … EXPANDING NESTED TABLES       KEEPING TARGET LINES   (7.56)

Example

For full example see class cl_demo_move_crrspndng_itab.

Given 2 internal tables

MOVE-CORRESPONDING itab1 TO itab2.

MOVE-CORRESPONDING itab1 TO itab2 KEEPING TARGET LINES.

MOVE-CORRESPONDING itab1 TO itab2 EXPANDING NESTED TABLES.

MOVE-CORRESPONDING itab1 TO itab2 EXPANDING NESTED TABLES
                                  KEEPING TARGET LINES.

 

7. LOOP AT……STEP n (7.57)

Description

STEP can be used with LOOP, FOR, APPEND, DELETE, INSERT, VALUE and NEW.

For LOOP and FOR, if n is negative, then the loop starts at the last line of the table and goes backwards with a step size of n.

Example

For full example see class cl_demo_loop_at_itab_using_stp.

DATA itab TYPE HASHED TABLE OF i WITH UNIQUE KEY table_line
WITH NON-UNIQUE SORTED KEY sec_key
COMPONENTS table_line
##TABKEY[PRIMARY_KEY][SEC_KEY].

itab = VALUE #( ( 4 ) ( 3 ) ( 7 ) ( 11 ) ( 1 ) ( 5 ) ).

LOOP AT itab ASSIGNING <fs> STEP 2.
    tabix = sy-tabix.
    result = VALUE #( BASE result ( tabix = tabix value = <fs> ) ).
ENDLOOP.

Result: result = [(0, 0, 0)  (4, 7, 1)]       "Note tabix is always 0.

 

LOOP AT itab ASSIGNING <fs> STEP -2.
    tabix = sy-tabix.
    result = VALUE #( BASE result ( tabix = tabix value = <fs> ) ).
ENDLOOP
Result:   result = [(0, 0, 0)  (5, 11, 3)]       "Note tabix is always 0.

 

 8. CORRESPONDING Operator Using the Additions MAPPING and DEFAULT  (7.58)

Syntax

CORRESPONDING #( struc1 MAPPING t1 = [s1] DEFAULT expr…)

Explanation

Where struc1 is the source structure and s1 is a component of the source structure.

When s1 is supplied the result of the expression (expr) is only assigned if s1 is initial. Else s1 is assigned.

When s1 is not supplied then the result of the expression is always assigned regardless of whether  s1 is initial.

Example

For full example see class cl_demo_corr_op_map_default.

DATA: BEGIN OF struc1,
    id1 TYPE i,
    a TYPE string,
    b TYPE string,
    c TYPE i,
    d TYPE string,
    e TYPE i,
END OF struc1.

DATA: BEGIN OF struc2,
    id2 TYPE i,
    a TYPE string,
    b TYPE string,
    c TYPE i,
    d TYPE string,
    z TYPE i,
END OF struc2.


"Empty internal table
DATA itab1 LIKE TABLE OF struc1 WITH EMPTY KEY.

"Filling structure
struc1 = VALUE #( id1 = 1 a = `a` b = `` c = 2 d = `` e = 0 ).
struc2 = CORRESPONDING #(
                  struc1 MAPPING id2 = id1
                                                 b = b DEFAULT `ha` && `llo`
                                                 c = c DEFAULT 1 + 5
                                                 d = d DEFAULT VALUE #( itab1[ 1 ]-d DEFAULT `hi` )
                                                 z = DEFAULT 9.

struc2:

Component

Value

 

ID2

1

 

a

a

 

b

hallo

src b is initial

c

2

src c is not initial

d

hi

itab1[1]-d invalid. Would shortdump without DEFAULT

z

9

src e is initial

entry.title: Re: Rework Production order with Quality inspection lot a...
entry.content:
Hello @Rishikesh1 , 

Thanks for your question. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, managing rework scenarios for serialized materials with quality inspections involves several steps to ensure that the serial numbers are maintained throughout the process. Here's a general approach to handle this scenario: 

1.Identify the Defective Material:  After the production order confirmation, the stock is posted to Quality Inspection (QI). In transaction QA32, you perform the usage decision and identify the defective material that needs rework.

2.Move to Blocked Stock: If defects are found, you move the defective quantity to the blocked stock, ensuring that the serial numbers are recorded and transferred along with the materials.

3.Create a Rework Order:

- Create a rework production order manually using transaction code CO07 or through the Manage Production Orders app.
- In the rework order, reference the original production order and the material to be reworked.
- Ensure that the order type is configured for rework and that it allows for the use of serialized materials.
- In the order, specify the serial numbers of the materials that need rework. This ensures that the same serial numbers are used throughout the rework process.

4. Perform the Rework:

- Release the rework order and perform the necessary operations to correct the defects.
- During confirmation of the rework order (using transaction CO11N or the Confirm Production Operations app), ensure that the serial numbers are referenced correctly.

5. Return to Quality Inspection:

- After rework confirmation, the system should automatically post the stock back to Quality Inspection with the same serial numbers.
- If this does not happen automatically, you may need to perform a stock transfer posting using transaction MIGO or the Post Goods Movement app, moving the stock from the rework location back to QI stock and ensuring that the serial numbers are included in the transfer.

6.Final Usage Decision:

- Once the reworked materials are back in QI, perform a new usage decision in QA32 to determine if the materials now meet the quality standards.
- If they pass the inspection, you can then move them to unrestricted use or to their intended stock location.

Important Notes:
- Ensure that the master data for the material is set up correctly to manage serial numbers throughout the process.
- The configuration of order types, material types, and plant settings can impact how serial numbers are managed in rework scenarios.
- Always test the process in a non-production environment to confirm that the serial numbers are maintained as expected before implementing it in the live system.

Please note that the exact steps and transactions may vary depending on the specific configuration and version of your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system.

Kind Regards

Dave

entry.title: Inspection Lot number cannot be displayed on the ERP Client
entry.content:
Hello,

I completed the ewm - qm integration in the decentralized s/4HANA system with the "How To Configure Quality Management Processes for Decentralized EWM Based On SAP S/4HANA" published by SAP. An inspection lot number was created in the decentralized EWM client, but this number cannot be displayed on the ERP side. None of the inspection lot numbers I created that day are in erp (even when I search without entering the inspection number). There is no error in the queue. When I look at slg1 in the ewm client, I do not see a record related to QIE. Delivery completion information went to ERP. What do you think I may have done wrong?

entry.title: How to load purchase orders in S/4HANA with Winshuttle
entry.content:
How to load purchase orders in S/4HANA with Winshuttle

 Purpose

This blog explains how to load purchase orders using the Winshuttle Studio with BAPI_PO_CREATE. I will show two cases, one for single document creation and another for mass migration. I will explain how create an script using a the SAP function module and what is the logic to fill the different sheets in the file template for purchase orders.

 

 

Build load file for Winshuttle Studio purchase order load – single document

Transaction ME21N having several items is difficult to use in recordings, so I will use a function module " BAPI_PO_CREATE1 ", which is used to create purchase orders in SAP.

It has import structures:

and also tables.

In case we just need to create one document we can handle it in most cases with just one sheet, which is more user friendly in order to fill the fields and do the upload. We can add buttons with VBA to call different winshuttle scripts and I will explain later. This is particular useful to avoid the user to enter SAP and fill many fields in several sheets.

In order to identify header and items in the Winshuttle script we need to use an identifier, which will be a hidden column with value “H” for header and “P” for item.

 

 

Build load file for Winshuttle Studio purchase order load – mass migration

For the case of mass migration we will have different sheets in order to fill the BAPI structures so we can enter as many documents as needed.

In Header we fill one line for each PO to be created. We have a column A with an identifier to link all sheets.

Item sheet:

Schedule lines:

Pricing conditions:

This follows similar logic to the load template provided by SAP for data migration cockpit.

You can add additional sheets for purchasing texts, accounting, …

 

Create the script with Winshuttle Studio v12.x

There are different methods to create an script or a query. We chose the direct input in which BAPIs or RFC can be used.

You need to logon to SAP in order to import the RFC structure

Choose RFC and fill the field:

Once selected, we find all tables to fill in the function module with the fields. We need to select those that are relevant for the load, which includes the mandatory tables/fields as also other fields that need to be filled in the load. We will need to check the filed “commit required” so the data is created in SAP.

 

Mapping for single document/sheet

In order to fill the tables for items, conditions, partner functions, etc. we need to use the header/position identifier.  Header will come with text “H” and item with text “P”

 

Mapping for multisheet/mass migration

If we have a mass migration with multiple sheets we need to have a column ID to link each record in the header with the ones in other sheets. Then we have to add a loop using the identifier (in column A).

We assign the values with the fields in the Excel file. The POHEADER has only one record for each line in the sheet. In table RETURN we assign the log messages sent by the function module.

We can do the same for schedule lines, pricing conditions, partner functions, etc.

 

Add VBA code to Excel to run scripts and other actions

 

We have the chance to use VBA code in order to perform activities in the Excel like filling sheets, clear cells and also call Winshuttle Scripts, so we use a button instead of the Winshuttle Studio Addon menu.

We add different modules with the necessary code in order to run the scripts. We can add information pop-ups, etc.

Run the script with Winshuttle Studio to create purchase orders

Before running the script, we can check the data with the winshuttle option in order to detect possible format or value errors as defined in the mapping. When ready, we can load the data online with the Run option or schedule a job.

If we have used VBA code to add button to call the scripts, that will make process more user friendly for the load, clear logs, download data to dropdown lists.

When we run the script, Winshuttle will write the log from table Return into the columns that we assigned. We get a clear message with possible errors or the purchase order number created.

Alternatives

In S4 HANA we can also use the Migration Cockpit (transaction LTMC). A template for purchase orders can be filled and the load can take place with this tool.

 

Summary

Winshuttle provides a useful solution to load purchase orders getting a detailed log with the errors or document numbers created. The advantages over data migration cockpit are a more flexible file build (only mandatory and relevant fields need to be added) and more detailed log, which is more user friendly. Additionally, if the script is embedded in the Excel file, the user is able to do preload validations or load without using the Winshuttle Studio, which makes the validation & load tasks much easier.

The use of VBA code makes possible to add extra functionality like buttons, call scripts directly, make data import, conversions, data extraction from SAP …

Using Winshuttle helps the user to fill only the relevant fields instead of going through the several screens in ME21N.

entry.title: Re: ABAP 7.5x Quick Reference
entry.content: This Quick reference is a collection of what are subjectively the highlights of ABAP functionality made available in ABAP 7.5x. The already good SAP examples are simplified/summarised to allow quicker understanding of the basics of a given new functionality. Links to the full SAP examples are also provided to allow the reader to dive in deeper if desired.

entry.title: Re: Payroll Canada Year End 2024
entry.content:
Dear All,

Provincial Tax Total credits and Federal Tax Total credits tables were updated effective 01.01.2024.

We have Bi weekly payroll.  01st payroll of 2024 was started from 23.12.2023 with pay date 12.01.2024

So users updated IT0462 and IT0463 manually as correct values were not getting picked.

First payroll of 2025 is starting from 21.12.2024 with pay date 10.01.2025 with support pack O2.

User expecting system to pick correct Provincial Tax Total Credits and Federal Tax Total Credits amount effective 01Payroll of 2025(payroll start date 21.12.2024 with pay date 10.01.2025) once support pack O3 is upgraded

Please let me know if system will pick correct Provincial Tax Total Credits and Federal Tax Total Credits values without updating IT0462 and IT0463 tables manually

Regards

Venkatesh
